_comment: DocNet configuration file. Copy this to docnet.config.json and edit for your project.

project._comment: Basic project identity – shown in the browser UI
project.name: My Document Network
project.description: A network visualization of relationships extracted from my document collection.
project._repoUrl_comment: Optional URL shown as a link in the sidebar header. Set to null to hide.
project._accentColor_comment: UI accent color. One of: blue, green, purple, orange, red

_comment: The 'principal' is the central entity in your document network.
The hop-distance filter measures how many relationship-steps separate
every other actor from this entity.
Set name to null to disable hop-distance filtering entirely.
This is the right choice when your corpus has no clear central figure,
or when you want to explore the full network without a focal point.
_aliases_comment: Alternative names, email addresses, or identifiers that refer to the principal.
The AI extractor will canonicalize all of these to principal.name in the database.
principal._hopFilterDefault_comment: Default value for the hop-distance slider in the UI (1–5, or null for 'Any').
_hopFilterEnabled_comment: Whether to show the hop-distance filter slider in the UI at all.
Set to false when principal.name is null, or when all actors
are equally important regardless of distance from the principal.

_model_comment: Claude model used for document analysis.
Use Haiku for large corpora (>500 docs), Sonnet for precision work.
_documentCategories_comment: The list of valid document categories injected into the AI prompt.
Corporate: memo, contract, board_minutes, audit_report, press_release
Legal: pleading, deposition, exhibit, affidavit, judgment
_yearRange_comment: The year range for the time-filter slider in the UI.
Set to the earliest and latest years plausibly found in your documents.
analysis._includeUndatedDefault_comment: Whether undated relationships are shown by default in the UI.

ui._comment: Text content shown in the frontend. Customize for your corpus.
ui.welcomeBody:
This is a network analysis tool for exploring relationships between people, places, and events extracted from a document collection.

AI was used to extract these relationships from the raw document text. Some errors and omissions are possible.

Click on a relationship in the timeline after selecting an actor to see the source document with highlighted entities.
howToUse: Search for actors using the search bar
Click on nodes in the graph to explore their relationships
Use filters to focus on specific content categories
Click document links in the timeline to view source documents
ui._defaultRelationshipLimit_comment: Number of relationships shown on desktop by default (100–25000).
ui._mobileRelationshipLimit_comment: Number of relationships shown on mobile by default.
ui.aiAttributionNote: Relationships were extracted using Claude AI. Verify important findings against source documents.
ui._aiAttributionNote_comment: Shown as a disclaimer in the welcome modal.

_comment: Settings for Variant E (Hybrid export). Unused in Variant A.
When enabled, the pipeline bundles outputs into a portable .docnet archive
that can be opened without a server.

server._comment: Server configuration. Can also be set via environment variables (env vars take priority).
